The supply of the currency will increase and the currency will appreciate.
The supply of the currency will decrease and the currency will appreciate.
The demand for the currency will decrease and the currency will appreciate.
The demand for the currency will increase and the currency will depreciate.

French imports from England will decrease.
Aggregate demand will increase in France.
Aggregate demand will increase in England.
Aggregate demand will decrease in England.

q:

An increase in aggregate demand because imports will decrease.
An increase in aggregate demand because exports will decrease.

The D curve begins on the vertical axis, just below dollars per pound, and moves downward and to the right in a straight line. o: amount of gold reserves held by the central bank in each country
gross domestic product of the exporting country
demand for and supply of each country’s currency
amount of resources available to the importing country
real interest rate in the exporting country

q:

An increase in the supply of the euro
An increase in the demand for the euro
An increase in the demand for the yen
A decrease in the supply of the yen

q:

Which of the following is true if the exchange rate between the euro and the dollar changes from $1.00 per euro to $1.25 per euro?
o: United States exports to Europe will increase.
European exports to the United States will increase.
The dollar has appreciated.
The euro has depreciated.
The money supply in the United States will increase.

Which is included in the current account

purchases of foreign bonds
purchases of foreign real estate
holdings of foreign currencies
exports of services

q:

The nation’s currency appreciating

Increasing taxes on exports

q:
Effect of increase in real interest rates in US

The supply of United States dollars in the foreign exchange market will increase.
The demand for United States dollars in the foreign exchange market will increase.
Net exports in the United States will increase.
The United States capital and financial account will be in deficit.

q:
Current account surplus, increased capital outflow

A decrease in the country’s national savings
A decrease in the country’s financial account deficit
An increase in the country’s net financial capital outflows
An increase in the country’s national debt

q:
Rising Xn creates balance of trade surplus
A country can have an increased surplus in its balance of trade as a result of
o: an increase in domestic inflation
declining imports and rising exports
higher tariffs imposed by its trading partners
an increase in financial capital inflow
an appreciating currency

q:
International bond purchase counts in CFA
Which of the following transactions is included in the financial account of Country X’s balance of payments accounts?
o: A firm in Country X sells robots to a firm in Country A.
Country X sends financial aid to Country B.
An individual in Country X receives dividend payments from a firm in Country C.
An individual in Country X sends money monthly to family members in Country D.
An individual in Country X buys new government bonds issued by Country E.

q:
Deficit spending raises i rates, decreases Xn
Country X’s government increases its spending without raising taxes. Which of the following is true about the effect on Country X’s real interest rates and its subsequent effect on Country X’s net exports?
o: Real interest rates increase and net exports increase.

Real interest rates increase with no change in net exports.
Real interest rates decrease and net exports increase.
Real interest rates decrease and net exports decrease.

q:
USD appreciates, net exports fall

increase and result in a surplus in the United States financial account
decrease and result in a surplus in the United States financial account
decrease and result in a deficit in the United States financial account
remain unchanged because the surplus in the current account will be offset by the deficit in the financial account

q:
Rising price level leads to currency depreciation

A decrease in the country’s money supply
